stem: The region in the first quadrant under the line \( y = [[k]]x + [[c]] \), from \( x=[[a]] \) to \( x=[[b]] \), is revolved about the \( x \)-axis. Find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: By the disk method, each cross-section perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis is a disk of radius \( y=[[k]]x+[[c]] \), so the volume is \( \pi\int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}([[k]]x+[[c]])^{2}\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This doubles the correct volume.
This treats the solid as a single cylinder using the radius at \( x=[[b]] \) only, instead of integrating the varying radius.
This squares the CHANGE in radius over the interval rather than integrating the radius squared.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region bounded by the line \( y=[[m]]x \), the horizontal line \( y=[[k]] \), and the \( y \)-axis, with \( 0 \le x \le [[n]] \), is revolved about the \( x \)-axis. Find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Using washers perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis, the outer radius is \( [[k]] \) and the inner radius is \( [[m]]x \), for \( 0\le x\le [[n]] \) (where the line meets \( y=[[k]] \)). The volume is \( \pi\int_{0}^{[[n]]}\left([[k]]^{2}-([[m]]x)^{2}\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This uses only the outer radius, as if the solid were a solid cylinder with no hole removed.
This halves the solid-cylinder (no-hole) volume, as if using a triangular cross-section instead of a circular washer.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region in the first quadrant under the line \( y = [[k]]x + [[c]] \), from \( x=[[a]] \) to \( x=[[b]] \), is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Use the shell method to find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Using cylindrical shells of radius \( x \), height \( [[k]]x+[[c]] \), and thickness \( dx \), the volume is \( 2\pi\int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}x([[k]]x+[[c]])\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of \( 2\pi \).
This mistakenly applies the disk method (about the \( x \)-axis) instead of the shell method requested about the \( y \)-axis.
This drops a factor of 2 from the shell circumference \( 2\pi x \), using \( \pi x \) instead.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region in the first quadrant to the left of the curve \( x = [[k]]y + [[c]] \), from \( y=[[p]] \) to \( y=[[q]] \), is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: By the disk method, each cross-section perpendicular to the \( y \)-axis is a disk of radius \( x=[[k]]y+[[c]] \), so the volume is \( \pi\int_{[[p]]}^{[[q]]}([[k]]y+[[c]])^{2}\,dy = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This doubles the correct volume.
This treats the solid as a single cylinder using the radius at \( y=[[q]] \) only, instead of integrating the varying radius.
This squares the CHANGE in radius over the interval rather than integrating the radius squared.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region enclosed between \( y=[[k]]x \) and \( y=x^{2} \), for \( 0 \le x \le [[b]] \), where the line lies above the parabola on this interval, is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Use the shell method to find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Using shells of radius \( x \), height \( ([[k]]x) - x^{2} \) (top curve minus bottom curve), and thickness \( dx \), the volume is \( 2\pi\int_{0}^{[[b]]}x\left([[k]]x - x^{2}\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of \( 2\pi \).
This uses the shell height at \( x=[[b]] \) for every shell, instead of integrating the varying height.
This drops a factor of 2 from the shell circumference \( 2\pi x \), using \( \pi x \) instead.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.
